    Information for authors

    Overview

    Whether you= 're a first-time author or a seasoned researcher, navigating the publi= shing process can be both exciting and complex. This collection of resource= s is designed to guide you every step of the way=E2=80=94from developing yo= ur manuscript and selecting the right journal, to understanding peer review= , open access options, and post-publication promotion. Explo= re practical tools, expert advice, and step-by-step guides tailored to help= you publish with confidence and impact.

    =20

    Your publishing journey

    =20
    1
    Step 1: Step 1 - Explore publishing guidance &= support
    • Read our handy guides, which give you a qui= ck overview of the publishing process at every stage, from choosing a journal to promotion.
    • Browse our How to Do Research = and Get Published webinars<= /a>, blogs and courses.
    2
    Step 2: Step 2 - Review your publishing options
    =
    • Learn more about publishing as subscri= ption or Open Access
    • Explore your Open Access options, including fe= es, funding, licensing & payment information.
    3
    Step 3: Step 3 - Choose a journal
    =
    • Unlock the potential of your research: Top reasons to publish with Sage.
    • Explore open call for papers within our Special Collections , Special Issues or in your relevant discipline across our journals= .
    • View the full list of Sage=  Journals use the Journal Recommender&n= bsp;to find a journal that fits your research, or consider submitting to=  S= age Path.
    4
    Step 4: Step 4 - Prepare your manuscript
    =
    • Learn more about how to prepare your paper for su= bmission.
    • Familiarize yourself with our editorial policies and our publication ethics po= licies.
    • Browse Sage Author Service= s available to make sure your research paper meets format, langu= age, and style requirements.
    5
    Step 5: Step 5 - Submit your manuscript
    =
    • Read Sage=E2=80=99s Authorship policy and understa= nd 'who' should submit.
    • Learn 'where', 'how' and 'what' to submit.<= /li>
    6
    Step 6: Step 6 - Understand peer review & p= roduction
    • Learn about what to expect during peer revi= ew, how to handle revisions, how to respond to reviewers, including a 'response to reviewers' template and Sage's = peer review policy.
    • Find out more about the production and post-pub= lication process: such as author e-prints, licensing and reviewing your pub= lished article.
    • Visit Sage Path=  if your initial submission was unsuccessful and you'd like help = transferring to an alternative journal.
    7
    Step 7: Step 7 - Promote your art= icle & build=E2=80=A8your network
    • Find resources to help improve the chances of your article being fou= nd, read, downloaded and cited =E2=80=93 of your article and you making= an impact. 
    • Explore caree= r and networking resources.
